Guide.Description: The Water solubility model is based on Artificial Neural Network (ANN) in which the modelled endpoint is a physical chemical property is based on the dataset retrieved from TEST software that consist of 5020 chemicals from the EPISuiteTM database (US EPA. EPI Suite, Version 4.0. US EPA, 2019 (accessed 5/21/09))
Guide.Model: The model has been developed by Alberto Manganaro, Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCSS Via Mario Negri 2, 20156. Milano, Italy alberto.manganaro@marionegri.it https://www.marionegri.it/ It has been described here: “Benfenati E, Roncaglioni A, Lombardo A, Manganaro A. Integrating QSAR, Read Across, and Screening Tools: The VEGAHUB Platform as an Example. Advances in Computational Toxicology; Springer; 20 19. p. 365 81.” For a general description of the VEGA software see: Benfenati E, Manganaro A, Gini G VEGA-QSAR: AI inside a platform for predictive toxicology Proceedings of the workshop "Popularize Artificial Intelligence 2013", December 5th 2013, Turin, Italy Published on CEUR Workshop Proceedings Vol-1107
Guide.Dataset: The model is based on the dataset retrieved from TEST software that consist of 5020 chemicals from the EPISuiteTM database (US EPA. EPI Suite, Version 4.0. US EPA.a, 2019 (accessed 5/21/09)) 09)). Chemicals with water solubilities exceeding 1,000,000 mg/L were omitted from the overall dataset. In addition, data were limited to data points that are within 10°C of 25°C. The protocol for the test varied through the decades, including limit test and up down procedure. No details about the protocol are associated to each experimental data point used for the modelling. Availability of the training set: Yes Data for the dependent variable for the training set: All Availability of external validation set: Yes Data for each dependent variable for the external validation set: All
Guide.Descriptors: The Artificial Neural Network (ANN) has been trained with the 'nnet' package in the software platform R and exported as a PMML, using DRAGON descriptors. Software name and version for the descriptor generation: DRAGON (v. 7.0). Calculation of several sets of molecular descriptors from molecular geometries (topological, geometrical, WHIM, 3D MoRSE, molecular profiles, etc.) Prof. R.Todeschini - distributed by Talete srl, via Pisani 13, 20124 Milano, Italy http://www.disat.unimib.it/chm The selected descriptors have been implemented in VEGA. VEGA (https://www.vegahub.eu/) The VEGA software provides QSAR models to predict tox, ecotox, environ, phys-chem and toxicokinetic properties of chemical substances. emilio.benfenati@marionegri.it The model uses 15 molecular descriptors calculated within the VEGA descriptors and based on the Dragon software calculation and definition: ALogP piPC8 SEigm MATS1v PW6 P_VSA_i_4 LogP nR10 CATS2D_4_DL BEH2p BIC3 GATS2m H-050 D/Dr3 CATS2D_7_DL An in-house tool developed in the R statistical platform has been used to select the best descriptors set and size to be employed for the final model. The approach was based on a forward selection technique: starting from the descriptor most correlated with the experimental data, at each iteration the descriptor leading to the best model (among all the available descriptors) was added, until the size of 25 descriptors. Models have been built, with a linear regression modelling, applied with a bootstrap cross-validation approach (n = 100). For each model, the fitness function has been calculated as the R squared coefficient obtained from the models built in each bootstrap iteration. This fitness function has been used to select the best descriptor to be added to proceed to the next iteration. From this procedure, the set of descriptors with the best cross-validation values has been chosen as the set to be used for the final model. The "best" values have been considered taking into account their trend: by progressively adding descriptors to the model, the cross-validation performances increase until a plateau (and, following, a decrease), which means that the optimal number of descriptors have been reached, and adding further descriptors would lead to over-fitting.

AD.Name: Similar_Molecules
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: strongly similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: only moderately similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: no similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found

AD.Name: Accuracy
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is good
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not optimal
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not adequate

AD.Name: Concordance
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that agree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value

AD.Name: Maximum_Error
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: the maximum error in prediction of similar molecules found in the training set has a low value, considering the experimental variability
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: the maximum error in prediction of similar molecules found in the training set has a moderate value, considering the experimental variability
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: the maximum error in prediction of similar molecules found in the training set has a high value, considering the experimental variability

AD.Name: Model_Descriptors_Range_Check
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: descriptors for this compound have values inside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: descriptors for this compound have values outside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set

AD.Name: ACF_SimCheck
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: all atom centered fragment of the compound have been found in the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some atom centered fragment of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: a prominent number of atom centered fragments of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments

AD.Name: AD_Index
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: predicted substance is into the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: predicted substance could be out of the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: predicted substance is out of the the Applicability Domain of the model

xml.Endpoint: The endpoint of the model is a physical- chemical property, the water solubility (P-CHEM, 4.8) (-Log(mol/L)).
